rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER K. RENFRO VS. AMERICAN APPAREL, INC., A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice On Petition And Petition O Vacate Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 22, 2011, line 1. DEDENDANTS AMERICAN APPAREL, INC., SEAN ALONZO Petition to Vacate Arbitration Award. Defendant's petition to vacate arbitration award is denied. It is clear from the record and transcript that the arbitrator considered the evidence and argument presented by defendant regarding the releases and rejected these releases as a defense. This matter will be called at the end of the Law and Motion Calendar. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: F. ALIOTO COMPANY, INC, A CALIFORNIA VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 22, 2011, line 2. DEFENDANT EXXONMOBIL OIL CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant Exxon/ Mobile¿s Motion for Summary Judgment/Adjudication is granted in part. Because Plaintiffs have pled causes of action for continuing nuisance and trespass only, Plaintiff is limited to damages for the three years proceeding this suit. Plaintiff¿s past damages before 6/29/06 and prospective damages are barred as a matter of law. Mangini v.Aerojet-General Corp. (1996) 12 Cal.4th 1087, 1093; Beck Dev. Co. v. So.Pac. Transp. Co. (1996) 44 Cal.App.4th 1160, 12176-17. The Motion for Summary Judgment/Summary Adjudication on the ground that the alleged damages are too speculative is denied. The Motion for Summary Judgment/Summary Adjudication on the grounds that the alleged damages for loss of access to Wharf J-10 is barred by collateral estoppel is denied. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: F. ALIOTO COMPANY, INC, A CALIFORNIA VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 22, 2011, line 2. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant CCSF¿s Motion for Summary Judgement/Adjudication is denied. As to the Res Judicata/Collateral Esptoppel arguments on the 4th and 5th causes of action, there was no reference in the prior action to ¿permanent¿ loss of access. The issues in the Court of Appeal decision involved the duty to inspect and repair under the Lease. By implication, a duty to repair means that the wharf must be repaired and, therefore, loss of access would not be permanent. Furthermore, the jury awarded Plaintiff $2.8 million for construction costs to rebuild the processing facility, also implying that the case was not about a permanent loss of access. As to the 4th cause of action for Breach of Contract, Plaintiff has submitted evidence that raises a triable issue of material fact as to several alleged obligations and breaches. As to the 5th cause of action for Breach of Implied Covenant of Quiet Enjoyment, Defendant has failed to show that a claim for breach of the covenant could not be established. There is plenty of evidence to establish triable issues of fact (Port named as dischargers of the pollution initially and later in time when it demolished the Wharf; the pollution has prevented Plaintiff from rebuilding its facilities.) =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL CORP VS. JAMES P QUINN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compelresponses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, And Request For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 22, 2011, line 3. PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S Motion To Compel responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, And Request For Production Of Documents. Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories, special interrogatories and request for production of documents is granted. Defendant's are directed to produce further responses by September 6, 2011. Plaintiff's request for monetary sanctions is granted in the amount of $2500. Defendants are directed to remit this amount to plaintiff's counsel by September 16, 2011. Request for issue and evidentiary sanctions denied. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: OLYMPIC VIEW REALTY, LLC VS. PARKMERCED INVESTORS PROPERTIES LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 22, 2011, line 4. PLAINTIFF OLYMPIC VIEW REALTY, LLC AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Plaintiff's motion for summary adjudication as to the 1st and 2nd causes of action is granted. Plaintiff has successfully met its burden. Defendant has failed to provide triable issues of fact regarding the definiteness of the contractual terms in question. The clear language of the contract does not support defendant's interpretation and the evidence shows defendant refused to execute new easements as required under the contrat. Moreover, plaintiff need not wait too incur actual damages from a breach of a contract. E.O.C. Ord., Inc. v. Kavakovich (1988) 200 Cal.App. 2d 220, 231. Plaintiff is entitled to an award of attorney fees but must present that by separate motion setting forth amounts to be awarded. =(302/LMG)
